rulings.Case Title: CARMEN E WARRI VS. MARGARET CHEN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Lift The Stay And Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 2, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANTS MARGARET CHEN, JORDAN HOROWITZ, CLINTON YOUNG, M.D.'S Motion To Lift The Stay And Dismiss Action. MOTION DENIED. THE 5-YEAR PERIOD IS TOLLED DURING THE STAY. [CCP SECTION 583.340(b).] BOUTWELL V. KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN (1988) 206 CAL.APP.3D 1371 DID NOT ADDRESS THIS PROVISION. BROCK V. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 1790 IS THE CONTROLLING AUTHORITY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY MENTO VS. UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE, A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 2, 2009, line 4, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY MENTO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. MOTION UNTIMELY SINCE DEFENDANT LOYALTY LABS GENERAL APPEARANCE WAS AUGUST 3, 2009. [CCP SECTION 437c(a).] MOTION DOES NOT ADDRESS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF ANY CAUSES OF ACTION. [CCP SECTION 437c(f)(1).] SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SEPARATELY IDENTIFY EACH CLAIM, CAUSE OF ACTION OR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. (CRC 3.1350) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER MCGARTY REGARDED PLAINTIFF'S COMMENTS AS A COMPLAINT. (MCGARTY DEPO, 40:18-20) PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SHOW THAT CO-DEFENDANT MEDINA ACTUALLY HARASSED PLAINTIFF AND HE DOES NOT DENY THAT HE HARASSED MEDINA. CO-DEFENDANT MEDINA HAS PROVIDED A DECLARATION THAT DISPUTES PLAINTIFF'S VERSION OF THE EVENTS. PLAINTIFF FIRST COMPLAINED OF HARASSMENT ON 4/4/08 BUT DID NOT WORK FOR UPS AFTER THAT DATE WHICH MOOTS FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE AND CORRECTIVE ACTION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO NEW HARASSMENT. MOTION TO BIFURCATE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO TRIAL COURT'S DETERMINATION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA BARNES VS. LESLIE BARHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 2, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF ANITA BARNES Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs. MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SUED UNDER S.F.R.O. SECTION 37.9(a)(8) AND IS THE PREVAILING PARTY, ENTITLING HER TO ATTORNEY FEES. [S.F.R.O. SECTION 37.9(f)]. THE CCP SECTION 998 OFFER DID NOT SPECIFICALLY EXCLUDE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS, THEREFORE THEY ARE NOT WAIVED. (ENGLE V. COPENBARGER & COPENBARGER (2007) 157 CAL.APP.4TH 165.) ATTORNEY FEES REASONABLE AND NECESSARY, AND ARE AWARDED IN THE LODESTAR AMOUNT OF $11,645 PLUS $3,137.05 COSTS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK L. WEBB VS. JULIA FOLLANSBEE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 2, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CAUSE OF ACTION IS BARRED BY ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. [CCP SECTION 340.6(a).] COURT GRANTS REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. REQUESTS FOR ORDER BARRING WEBB FROM FILING SUBSEQUENT LAWSUITS AND NOTIFYING STATE BAR OF WEBB'S ALLEGED VIOLATION OF PROBATION DENIED. =(302/CWW)
